page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-192 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the c4 family. It features 192 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 96 physical Intel Xeon 8581C cores operating at 2.3 GHz, utilizing hyperthreading with two threads per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It is equipped with 1488.0 GB of RAM, yielding 7.75 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark testing, the c4-highmem-192 demonstrates top-tier multi-core capabilities. It achieves top 10% performance in stress-ng multi-core CPU testing, PassMark CPU score, and PassMark database operations. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block and large block operations both ranking in the top 10%. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and PassMark memory scores falling into the middle 50% average category. The instance also exhibits strong capabilities in LLM inference, particularly in text generation for medium models and prompt processing for large models, which rank in the top 10% and top 25% respectively.

From a resource density perspective, the c4-highmem-192 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of nearly 8 GB per core, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The lack of local storage means all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes, which is a key architectural tradeoff. Given its high multi-threaded processing capacity, top-tier database operations performance, and substantial memory capacity, this instance is suited for large-scale database operations, memory-cached applications like Redis, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-192 is an x86_64 server instance powered by 192 dedicated vCPUs from Intel Xeon 8581C processors and 1488.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance relies entirely on network-attached storage. It delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and Redis SET operations. However, single-core CPU performance and PassMark memory scores remain in the average middle 50% range. This profile makes the instance highly effective for memory-intensive, highly parallelized workloads such as large database operations, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference, while presenting a tradeoff for applications requiring high single-threaded speed or local physical disks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-192 is an x86_64 server with 192 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1488 GB RAM. It lacks local storage but delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-192 features 192 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1488 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
tagline: A high-density memory instance featuring 192 dedicated vCPUs, optimized for massive database operations and parallelized memory-intensive workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8581C processors with 1488 GB RAM.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and high-speed memory bandwidth.
No local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring network-attached storage.
Excellent fit for large-scale database operations and Redis caching.
Strong performance in CPU-based LLM inference and software compilation.
